findings.message: New user-facing view `Dashboard` ships with no RUM/error instrumentation reachable from it. Unlike the RUM-wired app shell, this component renders a brand-new fetch-backed view whose load success/failure produces no RUM signal — the view's behavior is entirely unobserved.
findings.suggestion: Wrap the view in an error boundary (or surface failures through the js-errors path) and emit a RUM signal for the view load so the new user-visible behavior is observable, matching the instrumentation the rest of the app relies on via observability/rum.ts.

findings.message: New fetch path `fetch("/internal/summary")` has no error handling and no `res.ok` check (contrast api.ts which throws on non-ok). A non-2xx response does not reject, so the promise chain at lines 8-11 swallows the failure: `r.json()` is parsed/assigned with no error reaching the js-errors counter or window error handler. The failure becomes invisible — no RUM error signal is recorded for a failed dashboard load.
findings.suggestion: Check `res.ok` and throw on failure (as fetchItems does), and add a `.catch` that records a RUM error / re-throws to the unhandledrejection handler so failed loads of this view increment the js-errors counter instead of being silently dropped.
